clean_body: The 2025 General Assembly Session | | | | 2025 THE HONEY BADGER REPORT | | | | | | | 2025 SESSION Monday What can you expect? | ATTEND IN PERSON OR CALL IN TO THE MEETING LIVE | Monday & Tuesday Senate Committee Meetings | | | BILLS COMING UP MONDAY BY COMMITTEE CLICK THE LINKS BELOW FOR FULL LISTING OF ALL BILLS COMING UP TOMORROW | == == Patron: Feggans Secure storage of firearms; penalties. Creates a Class 4 misdemeanor for any person who fails to securely store a firearm on any premises where such person knows, or reasonably should know, that a minor or a person who is prohibited by law from possessing a firearm is, or is likely to be, present. The bill increases the penalty to a Class 1 misdemeanor if a minor or a person who is prohibited by law from possessing a firearm obtains such firearm. The bill includes certain exceptions and requires firearms dealers to post a notice stating firearm storage requirements and the penalty for improperly storing such firearms. The bill also creates a Class 4 misdemeanor for any person who fails to report to law enforcement the theft or loss of a firearm from a vehicle and a Class 1 misdemeanor if another person obtains such firearm. Finally, the bill requires the Superintendent of State Police, in conjunction with the Commissioner of Health, to create a public awareness campaign on the importance of the secure storage of firearms by January 1, 2026 == Patron: == Purchase, sale, transfer, etc., of assault firearms and certain ammunition feeding devices prohibited; penalties. Creates a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person who imports, sells, manufactures, purchases, or transfers an assault firearm, as that term is defined in the bill, and prohibits a person who has been convicted of such violation from purchasing, possessing, or transporting a firearm for a period of three years from the date of conviction. The bill provides that an assault firearm does not include any firearm that is an antique firearm, has been rendered permanently inoperable, is manually operated by bolt, pump, lever, or slide action, or was manufactured before July 1, 2025. The bill also prohibits the sale of a large capacity ammunition feeding device, as that term is defined in the bill. The bill provides that any person who willfully and intentionally (i) sells an assault firearm to another person or (ii) purchases an assault firearm from another person is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor and that any person who imports, sells, barters, or transfers a large capacity ammunition feeding device is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor. The bill also makes it a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person younger than 21 years of age to import, sell, manufacture, purchase, possess, transport, or transfer an assault firearm regardless of the date of manufacture of such assault firearm. == Patron: Helmer Firearm industry members; standards of responsible conduct; civil liability. Creates standards of responsible conduct for firearm industry members and requires such members to establish and implement reasonable controls regarding the manufacture, sale, distribution, use, and marketing of the firearm industry member's firearm-related products, as those terms are defined in the bill. Such reasonable controls include reasonable procedures, safeguards, and business practices that are designed to: (i) prevent the sale or distribution of a firearm-related product to a straw purchaser, a firearm trafficker, a person prohibited from possessing a firearm under state or federal law, or a person who the firearm industry member has reasonable cause to believe is at substantial risk of using a firearm-related product to harm themselves or unlawfully harm another or of unlawfully possessing or using a firearm-related product; (ii) prevent the loss of a firearm-related product or theft of a firearm-related product from a firearm industry member; (iii) ensure that the firearm industry member complies with all provisions of state and federal law and does not otherwise promote the unlawful manufacture, sale, possession, marketing, or use of a firearm-related product; and (iv) ensure that the firearm industry member does not engage in an act or practice in violation of the Virginia Consumer Protection Act. The bill also provides that a firearm industry member may not knowingly or recklessly create, maintain, or contribute to a public nuisance, as defined in the bill, through the sale, manufacturing, importing, or marketing of a firearm-related product. The bill creates a civil cause of action for the Attorney General or a local county or city attorney to enforce the provisions of the bill or for any person who has been injured as a result of a firearm industry member's violation to seek an injunction and to recover costs and damages == Patron: Laufer Firearm in unattended motor vehicle; civil penalty. Provides that no person shall leave, place, or store a handgun in an unattended motor vehicle, as defined in the bill, when such handgun is visible to any person who is outside such unattended motor vehicle. The bill provides that any person violating such prohibition is subject to a civil penalty of no more than $500 and that such unattended motor vehicle may be subject to removal for safekeeping. == Patron: Jones Trigger activator definition; penalty. Defines "trigger activator" as a conversion kit, tool, accessory, or device designed to alter the rate of fire of a semi-automatic firearm to mimic automatic weapon fire or used to increase the rate of fire to a rate faster than such semi-automatic firearm not equipped with a conversion kit, tool, accessory, or device. == Patron: Price Virginia Center for Firearm Violence Intervention and Prevention; Virginia Firearm Violence Intervention and Prevention Fund; creation. Creates the Virginia Center for Firearm Violence Intervention and Prevention within the Department of Criminal Justice Services. Establishes the Virginia Firearm Violence Intervention and Prevention Fund, to be administered by the Virginia Center for Firearm Violence Intervention and Prevention, to replace the existing Virginia Gun Violence Intervention and Prevention Fund that is administered by the Department. == Patron: Helmer Concealed handgun permits; reciprocity with other states. Provides that the Superintendent of State Police, in consultation with the Office of the Attorney General, shall determine whether states meet the statutory qualifications for Virginia to recognize the concealed handgun permit of a person from another state. Under current law, any out-of-state permit is recognized in the Commonwealth provided that (i) the issuing authority provides the means for instantaneous verification of the validity of all such permits or licenses issued within that state, accessible 24 hours a day; (ii) the permit or license holder carries a photo identification issued by a government agency of any state or by the U.S. Department of Defense or U.S. Department of State and displays the permit or license and such identification upon demand by a law-enforcement officer; and (iii) the permit or license holder has not previously had a Virginia concealed handgun permit revoked. The bill prevents a Virginia resident, except for an active duty service member or such service member's spouse, who has not been issued a valid resident concealed handgun permit from using a concealed handgun or concealed weapon permit or license issued by another state to carry a concealed handgun in the Commonwealth. The bill requires the Superintendent of State Police, in consultation with the Office of the Attorney General, to review any agreements for reciprocal recognition that are in place with any other states as of July 1, 2025, to determine whether the requirements and qualifications of those states' laws are adequate to prevent possession of a permit or license by persons who would be denied a permit in the Commonwealth and revoke any reciprocity agreement or recognition of any states that do not meet such requirements or qualifications by December 1, 2025. The bill requires the Attorney General to provide a written explanation for any determination that a state's laws are adequate to prevent possession of such permit or license by persons who would be denied such permit in the Commonwealth. == Patron: McClure Purchase, possession, or transportation of firearm; assault and battery of a family or household member or intimate partner; penalties. Adds to the definition of "family or household member," as such definition relates to juvenile and domestic relations district court, a person's intimate partner, defined in the bill as an individual who, within the previous 12 months, was in a romantic, dating, or sexual relationship with the person. The bill also adds to the definition of "family or household member," as such definition relates to a person's purchase, possession, or transportation of a firearm following an assault and battery of a family or household member, any individual who cohabits or who, within the previous 12 months, cohabitated with the person. Finally, the bill provides that any person who knowingly and intentionally purchases, possesses, or transports any firearm following a misdemeanor conviction for an offense that occurred on or after July 1, 2025, for the offense of assault and battery against an intimate partner or an offense substantially similar under the laws of any other state or of the United States is guilty of a Class 1 misdemeanor. == Patron: Callsen Carrying a firearm or explosive material within Capitol Square or building owned or leased by the Commonwealth; exemptions; public institutions of higher education; penalty. Permits the governing board of a public institution of higher education to adopt a policy prohibiting the carrying of any firearm, ammunition, or components or combination thereof within any building owned or operated by such public institution of higher education. Finally, the bill exempts certain activities, defined in the bill, operated at public or private institutions of higher education from any policy created by a governing board. == Patron: Bennett-Parker Firearm transfers to another person from a prohibited person. Provides that a person who is prohibited from possessing a firearm because such person is subject to a protective order or has been convicted of an assault and battery of a family or household member may transfer a firearm owned by such prohibited person to any person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm, provided that such person who is not otherwise prohibited by law from possessing such firearm is 21 years of age or older and does not reside with the person who is subject to the protective order. Under current law, there is no requirement that such transferee cannot be younger than 21 years of age and cannot reside with such prohibited person. The bill also provides that such prohibited person who transfers, sells, or surrenders a firearm pursuant to the provisions of the bill shall inform the clerk of the court of the name and address of the transferee, the federally licensed firearms dealer, or the law-enforcement agency in possession of the firearm and shall provide a copy of such form to the transferee. The bill also provides that a person who is prohibited from possessing a firearm because such person is subject to a protective order or has been convicted of an assault and battery of a family or household member shall be advised that a law-enforcement officer may obtain a search warrant to search for any firearms from such person if such law-enforcement officer has reason to believe that such person has not relinquished all firearms in his possession. == Patron: Hernandez Weapons; possession prohibited in a hospital that provides mental health services or developmental services; penalty. Makes it a Class 1 misdemeanor for any person to knowingly and intentionally possess in the building of any hospital that provides mental health services or developmental services in the Commonwealth, including an emergency department or other facility rendering emergency medical care, a (i) firearm, (ii) knife with a blade over three and one-half inches, or (iii) other dangerous weapon, including explosives and stun weapons. The bill also provides that notice of such prohibitions shall be posted conspicuously at each public entrance of any hospital and no person shall be convicted of the offense if such notice is not posted, unless such person had actual notice of the prohibitions. The bill provides that any such firearm, knife, explosive, or weapon shall be subject to seizure by a law-enforcement officer and forfeited to the Commonwealth and specifies exceptions to the prohibition. == Patron: Hayes Purchase of firearms; waiting period; penalty. Provides that no person shall sell a firearm unless at least five days have elapsed from the time the prospective purchaser completes the written consent form to have a licensed dealer obtain criminal history record information, with exceptions enumerated in relevant law. == Patron: Simon Placing swastika on certain property with intent to intimidate; penalty. | == == Patron: Hayes Charter; City of Portsmouth. Amends the charter for the City of Portsmouth by authorizing the City Manager to require city departments to implement appropriate enhancement or remedial measures consistent with prevailing law wherever there exists (i) a compelling government interest, (ii) an analysis that documents a statistically significant disparity between the availability and utilization of minority-owned and women-owned businesses, or (iii) direct evidence by minority-owned businesses or women-owned businesses of the impact of race by discrimination. == Patron: Martinez Disposable plastic bag tax; distribution to towns. Provides that any town located within a county that has imposed a disposable plastic bag tax shall receive a distribution of revenues collected by the county based on the local sales tax distribution formula for appropriations to towns. The bill requires that towns use such revenues for the same purposes allowable for a county or city. == Patron: Helmer Statewide housing targets for localities. Requires localities to increase their total housing stock by at least 7.5 percent over the five-year period beginning January 1, 2027. The bill provides that, in order to meet the 7.5 percent growth target, a locality shall develop a housing growth plan that best meets the needs of the locality and may include any of various listed housing growth strategies. The bill further provides that, after January 1, 2032, an applicant who seeks local government approval for a residential development that will have the effect of increasing the supply of housing in a locality and has that application rejected may, in addition to other remedies, appeal such decision to the Housing Approval Board, which shall be established by the Director of the Department of Housing and Community Development. The bill authorizes the Housing Approval Board to overturn local decisions and approve applications under certain circumstances. However, if the Housing Approval Board determines that a locality has in good faith implemented at least three of the housing growth strategies listed in the bill and has not rejected more than 25 percent of new housing development proposals over the previous five years, the Housing Approval Board shall allow the local decision to stand. Finally, the bill provides that the Housing Approval Board shall give extra weight for increases in affordable housing and for the rehabilitation of current, underutilized housing stock. The bill has a delayed effective date of July 1, 2026. | == == Patron: Carr Public institutions of higher education; student exchange programs; tuition and mandatory fee waivers; conditions. Clarifies that tuition and mandatory fees may be waived for a an inbound student enrolled in a public institution of higher education through a formalized student exchange program between such institution and another institution of higher education, provided that the number of inbound students does not exceed the number of outbound students paying full tuition and mandatory fees to the public institution of higher education under the provisions of the exchange program for a given three-year period. | == == Introduced by: == Board of Medicine; continuing education; unconscious bias and cultural competency. Directs the Board of Medicine to require unconscious bias and cultural competency training as part of the continuing education requirements for renewal of licensure. The bill specifies requirements for the training and requires the Board of Medicine to report on the training to the Department of Health and the Virginia Neonatal Perinatal Collaborative. == Introduced by: == School board policies; parental notification; safe storage of prescription drugs and firearms in the household. Requires each local school board to develop and implement a policy to require the annual notification of the parent of each student enrolled in the local school division, to be sent by email and, if applicable, SMS text message within 30 calendar days succeeding the first day of each school year, of (i) the importance of securely storing any prescription drug, as defined in relevant law, present in the household and (ii) the parent's legal responsibility to safely store any firearm present in the household. The bill requires each school board to make such parental notification available in multiple languages on its website. | == | == Patron: Feggans Department of Energy; Offshore Wind Industry Workforce Program and Fund. Directs the Director of the Department of Energy to identify and develop training resources to advance workforce development in the offshore wind industry in the Commonwealth. == Patron: Sullivan Electric Vehicle Rural Infrastructure Program and Fund created. Creates the Electric Vehicle Rural Infrastructure Program and Fund to assist private developers with non-utility costs associated with the installation of electric vehicle charging stations: (i) in certain localities, (ii) on eligible public land, as defined in the bill, or (iii) within one mile of the boundary of eligible public land. The bill provides that a private developer may apply for a grant in an amount equal to 70 percent of the private developer's non-utility costs for the installation of such public electric vehicle charging stations. The awarding of a grant under the program would be conditional upon an agreement with the applicant that any cost of a project not funded by a grant awarded by the program be funded by non-federal funds. The Department of Energy shall establish guidelines for the administration of the Program, including guidelines related to the application for and award of grants. The bill has an expiration date of July 1, 2030. == Patron: Ward Minimum wage. Increases the minimum wage incrementally to $15.00 per hour by January 1, 2027. The bill codifies the adjusted state hourly minimum wage of $12.41 per hour that is effective January 1, 2025 and increases the minimum wage to $13.50 per hour effective January 1, 2026 and to $15.00 per hour effective January 1, 2027. The bill requires the Commissioner of Labor and Industry to establish an adjusted state hourly minimum wage by October 1, 2027. | | Education and Health Subcommittee on Public Education | == Patron: Rasoul Public elementary and secondary schools; student discipline; student cell phone and smart devicepossession and use policies; development and implementation. Directs each school board to develop and each public elementary and secondary school to implement age-appropriate and developmentally appropriate policies relating to the possession and use of cell phones and smart devices by students on school property from bell to bell, as that term is defined in the bill. The bill requires such policies to: (i) restrict, to the fullest extent possible, student cell phone possession and use in the classroom from bell to bell; (ii) aim to reduce or prevent any distraction in or disruption to the learning environment, including bullying or harassment, that could be caused or facilitated by student cell phone possession and use on school property from bell to bell; (iii) ensure that implementation and enforcement of the policy is the responsibility of the administration, minimizes, to the extent possible, any conflict with the instructional responsibilities of teachers or any disruption to instructional time, and does not involve any school resource officer; (iv) include exceptions to such policies permitting any student, pursuant to an Individualized Education Plan or Section 504 Plan or if otherwise deemed appropriate by the school board, to possess and use a cell phone on school property, including in the classroom, during regular school hours to monitor or address a health concern; and (v) expressly prohibit any student from being suspended or expelled as a consequence of any violation of such policies. Finally, the bill clarifies that (a) no violation of any such student cell phone possession and use policy shall alone constitute sufficient cause for a student's suspension or expulsion from attendance at school and (b) any such violation that involves, coincides with, or results in an instance of disruptive behavior, as that term is defined in applicable law, shall be addressed in accordance with the regulations on codes of student conduct adopted by each school board pursuant to applicable law. == Patron: McQuinn Public schools; temporarily employed teachers; rules and requirements; extension of time limitation. Extends from 90 to 180 days the maximum period of time during one school year for which a school board may employ a temporarily employed teacher, as defined in relevant law, to fill a teacher vacancy, with certain conditions and restrictions. The bill has an expiration date of July 1, 2027. | AMANDA CHASE | VIRGINIA'S HONEY BADGER | Dear Hello Hope you had a great weekend because it's time to get back to work. Tomorrow there are a number of gun bills coming up, in violation of Article I Section 13 of the Virginia Constitution. We've seen these before, except by a Senate patron. Now you're seeing what is called a "House companion bill." This is simply the exact same bill, but it is introduced separately as a House bill. Most of these bills won't actually be heard again. The chair will simply comment that they've already debated and voted on a similar Senate bill along with the vote of that Senate companion and how the bill fared as a Senate bill. As a gun owner, I'm very concerned that these bills could become the law of the land next year if we don't take back the House and the Governor's office this November. Virginia has elections every year. We have to stay vigilant. I'm hopeful that just as miracles are occurring at the national level, we can have our own miracle right here in Virginia in 2025. | | It's hard to believe it, but next Saturday, February 22 is the last day of session. That said, we will turn our attention to the budget. As you may or may not know, by law legislators are required to balance the budget. I've included a copy below for you to review. Both the House and Senate will each vote on and pass a budget in their respective bodies before coming together in a Conference Committee to hash out the differences between the two budgets. Stay tuned as I start going through the budget now through next Saturday. | | | | | | | | | IMPORTANT DATES | 2025 LEGISLATIVE SESSION IMPORTANT DATES Begins: Wednesday, January 8 at noon Crossover: Tuesday, February 4 Ends: Saturday, February 22 Last Day for Governor's Actions: Monday, March 24 Reconvene: Wednesday, April 2 | IMPORTANT LINKS | | == | | == | | == | | == | | == | == | | == | SUPPORT AMANDA HERE | While this publication is free, your contributions are greatly appreciated. As long as my costs are covered, I can continue to provide this service to you. For starters, my email service costs about $300 per month. So please help out financially if you can. I do my own research and writing. So I promise there will be typos. Thanks in advance to all of my grammar hawks for your grace regarding this. I'm currently publishing this on a voluntary basis and to help you hold your government accountable. While this is a labor of love, I do have hundreds of dollars in expense each month to bring this service to you. Any contribution is greatly appreciated to offset the cost of providing it. I want to thank those of you in advance who have already contributed. I know who you are and know how much I appreciate your financial support. Thank you in advance. | | == | == == | Authorized and Paid for by Friends of Amanda Chase | | | | Friends of Amanda Chase | P.O. Box 250 | Appomattox, VA 24522 US | | | | | | |

GOP Rep. Byron Donalds **[told a woke CNN interviewer the blunt truth](https://substack.com/redirect/80f1a0fd-882b-4906-aa56-6cde1bc46ebf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** about President Donald Trump's plan to "find the rot" in the FBI and remove it immediately.
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/80f1a0fd-882b-4906-aa56-6cde1bc46ebf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
[](https://substack.com/redirect/80f1a0fd-882b-4906-aa56-6cde1bc46ebf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The CNN host pressed Donalds about Trump's plan to fire January 6 prosecutors, especially the rank and file agents who were merely following orders from hire-ups, "Why should they be given their walking papers?!!"
Donalds clearly explained that this has been Trump's goal from the start, "Trump talked about this, that he was going to clean out the rot that is in the Department of Justice, in the FBI, and so this is the beginning of that process."
"The political brass, to your point, is where a lot of these decisions were made," Donalds agrees, explaining that the rank and file agents will fall under a seperate protocol for review. "But you have to start somewhere!"
"These institutions have been rotting and **[been getting off of their core mission,](https://substack.com/redirect/80f1a0fd-882b-4906-aa56-6cde1bc46ebf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** especially over the last 4 years, but we can go as far back as the Obama administration!" Donalds added.
Watch the tense back-and-forth right here:
[Watch Video](https://substack.com/redirect/80f1a0fd-882b-4906-aa56-6cde1bc46ebf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

# Trump says he won't deport Prince Harry: "He's got enough problems with his wife..."
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/27a26c2c-959e-43bb-b02a-a6970780b77f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
President Donald Trump has reportedly ruled out deporting Prince Harry, while taking a savage jab at his infamous wife Meghan Markle.
Prince Harry's immigration status has come into question due to his admission of drug use in his memoir. Trump **[told the New York Post in a statement](https://substack.com/redirect/27a26c2c-959e-43bb-b02a-a6970780b77f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that he's got bigger things to worry about than deporting the British royal.
"I don't want to do that," Trump said. "I'll leave him alone. He's got enough problems with his wife. She's terrible."
The statement comes amid legal battles involving Prince Harry, who is challenging the U.S. Homeland Security's decision not to release his immigration records. Harry admitted to using cocaine, marijuana, and psychedelics, potentially affecting his visa status under U.S. law.
Trump's comments also follow his previous criticism of the couple during his presidency, where he labeled them "very disrespectful" after they publicly criticized the royal family.
* * *

Peter,

Wow! This email received an incredible response! If you missed it, we’ve included it at the bottom of today’s email. We’re so happy to hear that many of you feel we are on the right track to combat Trump’s misogynistic agenda.

Please keep your responses coming! We read as many of them as we can and sometimes even implement your constructive criticism...

We know you’re emotionally, physically, mentally, and even monetarily drained. But we want you to know that, at Her Bold Move, we are standing STRONG. We will keep fighting for as long as it takes – and, lucky for us, there are amazing, qualified, pro-choice women running for offices across the country RIGHT NOW.

[**Help us make our plan happen ASAP! Let’s defend that hard-fought progress on behalf of our mothers, our grandmothers, our daughters, and our friends. Take a moment to join our fight today by donating $20.25 or more immediately. →**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101848162/527254959/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_250210fwd_N_JZ_90DO_15_NG_N_L1&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIwYjhlZjM5LTMxZTctZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=noas3uDOdip_Bz0k0zVvOyT5dUl3T1ZaxZVWT-XM0Co=)

In this together,

Jordan Zaslow
Founder and President
Her Bold Move

\----------- Forwarded message ---------

Hi Peter,

We’re hearing from so many of you – more than ever before – that the news these past few weeks has been overwhelming.

We understand how you feel. There’s an open misogynist and sexual predator in the White House, our Secretary of Defense, Pete Hegseth, reportedly believes that “women should not _vote or work_ ,” Elon Musk appears to have installed “an army of 20-something men” to dismantle U.S. agencies, male legislators across America are sponsoring bills that criminalize women for accessing life-saving reproductive healthcare... and so much more.

We thought today would be a good time to share our plan to protect – and even expand – women’s political power over the next four years.

[**But first, consider chipping in $20.25 or whatever you can to help us elect more pro-choice, pro-women WOMEN to protect American women’s rights and safety over the next four years.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101848163/527254961/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_250210fwd_N_JZ_90DO_15_NG_N_L2&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIwYjhlZjM5LTMxZTctZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=noas3uDOdip_Bz0k0zVvOyT5dUl3T1ZaxZVWT-XM0Co=)

**We have an exciting twofold strategy to increase women’s political power and improve representation starting THIS YEAR:**

1\. **We’re going to support women candidates in _underserved races_ :** These are the races you might not hear about on the news – but the outcomes impact the lives of millions of Americans. We don’t want ANY women to get left behind – no matter where they live. We’re not afraid to support candidates running in red, abortion-ban states or other unexpected parts of the country. (As you’ll see below, we know how to win in those places.)

2\. **Build infrastructure in underserved places:** The states with abortion bans happen to be the same states in which it’s incredibly difficult to run for office as a Democrat and win – and even harder to run for office as a pro-choice WOMAN and win. But the voters in those states deserve better. We’re building much-needed infrastructure in those parts of the country to serve as a strong foundation for pro-choice women launching a campaign.

You may call us crazy. American Democrats have learned to accept a status quo that means we simply won’t win in red states. Well, we’re excited to report that last cycle, 44% of Her Bold Move VICTORIES came out of RED STATES.

We believe there are plenty of reasons to be optimistic.

At Her Bold Move, we are standing strong.

[**Help us make our plan happen ASAP! Let’s defend that hard-fought progress on behalf of our mothers, our grandmothers, our daughters, and our friends. Take a moment to join our fight today by donating $20.25 or more immediately. →**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101848164/527254962/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_250210fwd_N_JZ_90DO_15_NG_N_L3&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIwYjhlZjM5LTMxZTctZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=noas3uDOdip_Bz0k0zVvOyT5dUl3T1ZaxZVWT-XM0Co=) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | [**$20.25 »**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101848165/527254963/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_250210fwd_N_JZ_90DO_15_NG_N_EB20.25&express_lane=true&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIwYjhlZjM5LTMxZTctZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=noas3uDOdip_Bz0k0zVvOyT5dUl3T1ZaxZVWT-XM0Co=)

Onward,

Jordan Zaslow
Founder and President
Her Bold Move
| | Her Bold Move is the fastest-growing support network for women running for office in the United States. Since our founding in 2020, we have supported hundreds of pro-choice, pro-women women candidates across 42 states and counting. As abortion bans and other dangerous laws about women tend to get passed in states with the lowest representation numbers for women in government (4%, 11%, 12%....) we've made it our mission to break the anti-choice male supermajorities in those state legislatures.

**Support this work with a contribution today!** [DONATE!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101848171/527254969/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_250210fwd_N_JZ_90DO_15_NG_N_biobox&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImIwYjhlZjM5LTMxZTctZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=noas3uDOdip_Bz0k0zVvOyT5dUl3T1ZaxZVWT-XM0Co=)

clean_body: | | | | | = Peter, Wow! This email received an incredible response! If you missed it, we’ve included it at the bottom of today’s email. We’re so happy to hear that many of you feel we are on the right track to combat Trump’s misogynistic agenda. Please keep your responses coming! We read as many of them as we can and sometimes even implement your constructive criticism... We know you’re emotionally, physically, mentally, and even monetarily drained. But we want you to know that, at Her Bold Move, we are standing STRONG. We will keep fighting for as long as it takes – and, lucky for us, there are amazing, qualified, pro-choice women running for offices across the country RIGHT NOW. = In this together, Jordan Zaslow Founder and President Her Bold Move \----------- Forwarded message --------- Hi Peter, We’re hearing from so many of you – more than ever before – that the news these past few weeks has been overwhelming. We understand how you feel. There’s an open misogynist and sexual predator in the White House, our Secretary of Defense, Pete Hegseth, reportedly believes that “women should not vote or work ,” Elon Musk appears to have installed “an army of 20-something men” to dismantle U.S. agencies, male legislators across America are sponsoring bills that criminalize women for accessing life-saving reproductive healthcare... and so much more. We thought today would be a good time to share our plan to protect – and even expand – women’s political power over the next four years. = We have an exciting twofold strategy to increase women’s political power and improve representation starting THIS YEAR: 1\. We’re going to support women candidates in underserved races : These are the races you might not hear about on the news – but the outcomes impact the lives of millions of Americans. We don’t want ANY women to get left behind – no matter where they live. We’re not afraid to support candidates running in red, abortion-ban states or other unexpected parts of the country. (As you’ll see below, we know how to win in those places.) 2\. Build infrastructure in underserved places: The states with abortion bans happen to be the same states in which it’s incredibly difficult to run for office as a Democrat and win – and even harder to run for office as a pro-choice WOMAN and win. But the voters in those states deserve better. We’re building much-needed infrastructure in those parts of the country to serve as a strong foundation for pro-choice women launching a campaign. You may call us crazy. American Democrats have learned to accept a status quo that means we simply won’t win in red states. Well, we’re excited to report that last cycle, 44% of Her Bold Move VICTORIES came out of RED STATES. We believe there are plenty of reasons to be optimistic. # [#]
Peter: You’ve probably seen the news about Trump’s executive orders:

🔴 Ending birthright citizenship which is in DIRECT opposition to the 14th Amendment

🔴 Eliminating certain environmental justice programs that serve Black and Latino communities

🔴 Terminating all diversity, equity, and inclusion programs across the federal government — impacting thousands of federal jobs

And these are only a few out of MANY executive orders.

That’s why we need to hear from you: do you support Trump’s latest executive orders targeting Black and Latino communities? Respond to our poll now! [https://secure.everyaction.com/zvWZ1LDqi0qoppf9C4hrNg2]

https://secure.everyaction.com/zvWZ1LDqi0qoppf9C4hrNg2 [https://secure.everyaction.com/zvWZ1LDqi0qoppf9C4hrNg2]It’s going to be a long four years. But together, we can fight for a state and nation that works for ALL of us, regardless of race, income, gender, preferred language, or ZIP Code.

name: Art @ All On The Line
subject: Read this during halftime
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
Finger foods and fighting for democracy.
|
---

Hi Peter!
If you’re anything like me, today is a day for serious snacking, top-tier commercials, and yelling at the TV (The Big Game brings out the best in all of us, right?).
While we also look ahead to the halftime show, we’d be remiss if we didn’t mention that we're _halfway_ through the decade — and we’ve already seen multiple mid-decade map redraws. Traditionally, redistricting would happen once every 10 years after the census.
**But now, there is no “halftime” in redistricting; no breaks. We have to play all the way through the decade to the next cycle. ****But just because the cards are stacked against us doesn’t mean we’ll back down without a fight.**
We’re facing more map-making battles (including Ohio this year!) and defending the 2030 Census from being undermined by the Trump administration. And on top of that, our affiliate is in court advocating for fair representation in Georgia, Alabama, and Florida.
Tackling gerrymandering year after year requires a strong, well-resourced team.
[**Will you rush a $21.50 donation right away to help make the promise of democracy real for every American? That’s _half_ the estimated amount the average fan is expected to spend on food and drinks for this year’s game. And 100% of every contribution brings voters closer to having fair maps and fair representation.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/101848454/527255950/508201740?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_SuperBowl_email_20250209-AOTL-Tier1-SuperBowl_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250209-AOTL-Tier1-SuperBowl_&utm_campaign=SuperBowl&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogImE2YjhlZjM5LTMxZTctZWYxMS05MGNiLTAwMjI0ODJhOTRmNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=6yNlwANpgwGbg715u4owSMDoJCOcZhGoZUaoHt00cac=)

clean_body: Finger foods and fighting for democracy. | Hi Peter! If you’re anything like me, today is a day for serious snacking, top-tier commercials, and yelling at the TV (The Big Game brings out the best in all of us, right?). While we also look ahead to the halftime show, we’d be remiss if we didn’t mention that we're halfway through the decade — and we’ve already seen multiple mid-decade map redraws. Traditionally, redistricting would happen once every 10 years after the census. But now, there is no “halftime” in redistricting; no breaks. We have to play all the way through the decade to the next cycle. But just because the cards are stacked against us doesn’t mean we’ll back down without a fight. We’re facing more map-making battles (including Ohio this year!) and defending the 2030 Census from being undermined by the Trump administration. And on top of that, our affiliate is in court advocating for fair representation in Georgia, Alabama, and Florida. Tackling gerrymandering year after year requires a strong, well-resourced team. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thanks! Art Mitchell Deputy Director of Litigation, All On The Line = is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. Support our work to end gerrymandering. VERY few people are paying attention to this...

**And it could endanger President Trump 's entire legislative agenda!**

By all accounts, Elise Stefanik's confirmation to Trump's Cabinet will sail through. She's an excellent pick - no surprise there. That tees up a Special Election in New York to replace her. Then, the Governor is mandated by state law to lay out the election calendar.

Here's where things get ugly.

**New York Democrats are floating a scheme to DELAY the Special Election. Why would they do that, you ask? Well... it might be because it's a Conservative-leaning district, and Republicans have a _razor-thin_ margin in the House. **

_Losing one seat - or even postponing a Republican victory - will have serious ramifications for President Trump's agenda to pass through Congress. _

Democrats are so desperate to stop President Trump that they're willing to rewrite state law AND deny representation in Congress to hundreds of thousands of Americans.

What a disgrace.

_**[I won't forget this. Neither should you. clean_body: Democrats are so desperate to stop President Trump that they’re willing to rewrite state law AND deny representation in Congress to hundreds of thousands of Americans. And it could endanger President Trump’s entire legislative agenda! | | | | | | "Majority Leader Steve Scalise" VERY few people are paying attention to this... And it could endanger President Trump 's entire legislative agenda! By all accounts, Elise Stefanik's confirmation to Trump's Cabinet will sail through. She's an excellent pick - no surprise there. That tees up a Special Election in New York to replace her. Then, the Governor is mandated by state law to lay out the election calendar. Here's where things get ugly. New York Democrats are floating a scheme to DELAY the Special Election. Why would they do that, you ask? Well... it might be because it's a Conservative-leaning district, and Republicans have a razor-thin margin in the House. Losing one seat - or even postponing a Republican victory - will have serious ramifications for President Trump's agenda to pass through Congress. Democrats are so desperate to stop President Trump that they're willing to rewrite state law AND deny representation in Congress to hundreds of thousands of Americans. What a disgrace. "I won’t forget this. Neither should you. That’s why I’m launching an emergency fundraising blitz to fight back and save our House Majority." I set a $100,000 goal and I'd really appreciate it if you'd be willing to pitch in a few bucks today. | | "CONTRIBUTE NOW" Let me be very clear: Passing President Trump's agenda is at the very top of my priority list. We need to get this right and time is of the essence. Every seat matters a LOT when the margins are so close. I won't stand around while a bunch of New York Democrats are trying to hamstring President Trump's entire legislative agenda. "Can you please help us? I really need your support right now. It doesn’t get more important than this. " Thanks for all you do, Leader Scalise | | | "CONTRIBUTE NOW" | | | "Majority Leader Survey" A personal note from Majority Leader Steve Scalise: I'm humbled to announce that I am your new House Majority Leader. My first order of business is checking in with our best supporters, so I just launched my new MAJORITY LEADER SURVEY for that specific reason. This is Black MAGA Patriot Byron Donalds
I’m the proud son of a single-mother from the inner-city who achieved the American Dream.
But when the “tolerant” left found out I’m a Black Man who supports President Trump, they opened-up the racist floodgates against me.
From ridiculous slurs to calling me a “white supremacist”, these people go NUTS when I break all of their outdated narratives on race.
**Look, I don’t care what your skin color is. If you love our country, you’re a friend in my book.**
But now radical Democrats are coming after me for my message on Black History Month, and sadly things are taking a turn for the worse for me.
They want each and every one of Trump’s Black MAGA supporters like me out of the picture, and they’re raising MILLIONS to see it happen next election.
I hate to admit this - but I’m at the top of their “ _**Must-Go MAGA List**_ ”, and it’s not looking good.
I’m fighting day and night for President Trump & The MAGA Movement, and I’m afraid their dreams of a Radical Left House Majority will come true if I can’t muster an overwhelming response this month.
Please, I can’t let President Trump down. You’re the only reason I’m able to keep fighting. _**[So if you can, could I count on your support?](http://links.conservativeum.com/s/c/sf7GpDHVNTcrVJRzC1YgfDlUQIHxroQPKCxbULlcLSuQQ_GWxYORcMLVBtvbjAERdnMkzrmURwvJ36wSci1XxSC5ODyJs39oBXfi2I_UdHG25-GY3rPS6jdQZudKOpiIE0I7FbajcyLr94ySRrChiHbb39px2GYemZHXX698Wuqa34rEIW13mTjecWEYut6XS1eKYZXpDMOfPdAkTXlbWRAbvZeybI3KtNrJI8Uxa3gjnXcABJWCBELZOp8x0OUOUE3so3t_PbwAlfM0zY2HqsD8LyyH-MZBCLP4ehcGxaD1mkZnecQaN8L_5MFt0E3QLdzU3A9hQ46kcUjvbzP7Y30EyAvcK4arQC1hEgNczjRXDm4NybqdX_tCgESXU4ODX6xaiTdf3dVuCu3jNCYJKN-sfGtw-9kHLUaVZaQhfZfJTzJcyeznu9P1Ko_3v2Rvs0o5mYt3MPZoMjB2Bcm1MW9ydgAFUxMinUZd3q6LvAnyyR6ySFTMQT3z6rpA420PLTmIXSAE2an3oiWjw4w6iXxUOY6iMrHrZLf711dEj4m7ifdnz0U3/8BNjTsZVdxycW7VGJaZhmgNME2zZCE72/5)**_

That’s why we’ve sent out an **urgent Social Security poll for your area****!**

name: Ruben Gallego
subject: DOGE + VA
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
Nobody elected these people, they’re operating without any statutory authority, and they’re failing to meet even the most basic demands of transparency.
#

Peter, we’ve been getting mixed reports that Elon’s interns are pushing their way into the VA, working out of the same playbook they’ve used at Treasury, USAID, OPM, OMB, and elsewhere.
Those reports started being confirmed last week.
Look — I’m not here to defend the shortcomings of our institutions. I know them better than most. I had to fight my way through the VA to get services and benefits for my brothers after a punishing deployment to Iraq.
But as Elon and his wonderkids demand mass resignations at federal agencies, how many VA nurses, doctors, counselors, and caseworkers are they forcing out? When they access payment systems, how many veterans are going to see their benefits cut? What happens when AI starts doling out automated disability ratings for veterans that don’t come close to what they’re owed?
Nobody elected these people, they’re operating without any statutory authority, and they’re failing to meet even the most basic demands of transparency.
I’m doing everything I can to get accountability and hold the line. I give you my word on that.
But the truth is, it’s going to take a massive movement to stop Elon Musk from dismantling a system that millions of veterans depend on. That means I need you speaking out.
[**I’m asking you to join me. Add your name and tell Elon Musk to get his hands off the Department of Veterans Affairs.**](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/signup/pet_em_250209_VA/?source=em250209-1-120&akid=3701%2E734936%2EJpsAqQ)
[SIGN ON NOW](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/signup/pet_em_250209_VA/?source=em250209-1-120&akid=3701%2E734936%2EJpsAqQ)
Thanks, Peter.
And Semper Fi.
Ruben

Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
Use of military rank, job titles, and photographs in uniform do not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or the Department of Defense.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails like this are a critical tool for our campaign's fundraising and organizing efforts. Or, if you need to, you can [unsubscribe here](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=2&akid=3701%2E734936%2EJpsAqQ). clean_body: Elon Musk, Don Jr., & JD Vance have been trying to reach you. | | | | Friend, are your notifications turned off? FIRST, Donald Trump Jr. reached out to you to take the Official MAGA Census , but you never responded. NEXT, JD Vance followed up, but you still haven’t responded. NOW, Elon Musk reached out to President Trump to make sure we got your response! Friend, this is your last chance to take the Official MAGA Census before we SHUT IT DOWN to finalize our internal records. There’s a reason we’re reaching out to you over anyone else in your hometown. Reaching out directly to grassroots leaders is expensive, so we can only afford to reach out to President Trump’s most reliable supporters. That’s why your response will be used to represent every one of your friends and neighbors...THIS IS IMPORTANT. | | | | | Contributions to Trump National Committee JFC Inc. are not deductible for federal income tax purposes. name: Pat Herrity
subject: Herrity Headlines
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Friends of Pat Herrity
body:
|
---
| |
---
|
---
| | Peter,

Once again, this week took my team and I to gatherings of Republicans across the Commonwealth as we work to continue connecting with supporters new and old and spreading the word about my campaign to continue commonsense, conservative leadership in Richmond.

---
| |
---
Our first stop this week was in Fauquier County, where I had the opportunity to speak with a fired up group of conservatives who are eager to continue improving on Republican performance in Virginia this year.
---
| |
---
I joined a great Republican Women's club with members from Winchester, Frederick County, and Clarke County for lunch in Winchester, and spoke about the importance of defending our conservative values through commonsense, conservative policy solutions.
---
| |
---
From there I made my way back to the Eastern Shore for a second time, joining the Northampton Republican Committee to talk about my campaign and learn more about the issues facing their region of the Commonwealth.
---
| |
---

The next morning I had the opportunity to speak and take questions at a breakfast for Republicans in Newport News. We talked about the importance of messaging our conservative priorities in a way that resonates with everyday people and delivers results for Virginia's families.
---
| |
---
That evening I joined the York County Republican Committee, a great group of friends and folks who have advanced our party by sending strong conservative leaders to Richmond the last few years, and are committed to keeping our statewide offices in Republican hands this year.
---
| |
---

This weekend I had the opportunity to catch up with old friends and new at the annual Mount Vernon district town hall in Fairfax County.
---
| |
---

That afternoon, I stopped through Loudoun County for a candidate petition drive and met with dozens of voters stopping through to sign their names to get our state and local candidates on the ballot for this year.
---
| If you haven't yet had the chance to get involved, we could use your help with collecting signatures from registered voters to ensure that my name is listed on the ballot!

Please send us an email asking for the forms, and we'll send you the forms along with all the instructions and information you need to collect signatures from your friends and neighbors!

---
| [Click Here to Request Forms](mailto:info@patforva.com?subject=I'd%20like%20to%20help%20Pat%20get%20on%20the%20ballot!&body=Please%20send%20me%20ballot%20access%20petition%20forms%2C%20and%20instructions%20on%20how%20to%20circulate%20them! "Click Here to Request Forms")
---
|
---
| I was honored to announce additional exciting endorsements this week from current and former elected officials, including former Congresswoman Thelma Drake, who represented the 2nd Congressional District, and Prince William County Supervisor Tom Gordy, who represents the Brentsville District.
---
| |
---

Representative Thelma Drake served the voters of Virginia Beach for decades, and as a Member of Congress until 2009. I'm honored to have her backing my campaign as we continue to work to earn the support of those in the Hampton Roads area.
---
| |
---

Supervisor Tom Gordy serves the people of Brentsville on the Prince William County Board of Supervisors, and his support of my campaign will make a huge impact in Western Prince William, across the county, and across Northern Virginia.
---
|
---
| Back home in Fairfax, I continued to hold my liberal counterparts accountable for decisions that put our community at risk.

This week, news broke that a registered sex offender previously identified in Arlington County, also [exposed himself to women and young girls in Fairfax County facilities](https://patherrity.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=deffacbf7c4eb8be8b9889258&id=9ce2187c97&e=a1ea0f9c99).

Fairfax County policy allows individuals to use the bathroom or locker room that aligns with their gender identity - whatever gender they say that is.

I had the opportunity to speak with the family of one of those young girls, whose grandmother has been a long-time friend and supporter. The young girl is still traumatized from the incident. She had to lock herself in a restroom stall until she felt it was safe to run out of the locker room.

Unfortunately, the experience she had was enabled by Fairfax County policy, which allows people to choose the bathrooms and locker rooms that they use based on the gender they identify with that day.

That policy emboldens people like the sex offender here, who take advantage of the opportunity to prey on women and young girls in our community. They put the security of women and young girls in all of our county facilities at risk and ultimately take away a woman or girls' ability to use our facilities at all without having to worry about their own safety or use the limited family changing rooms.

It’s time to return to common sense. Biological men have no place in women’s bathrooms or locker rooms, and I have urged my colleagues on the Board of Supervisors to work with me to update our policies and protect our women and children.

What's worse - [Steve Descano, our Commonwealth's Attorney in Fairfax, had previously declined to prosecute this registered sex offender](https://patherrity.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=deffacbf7c4eb8be8b9889258&id=fa8cae2681&e=a1ea0f9c99), allowing him to continue putting women and young girls at risk.

To compound matters, Steve Descano's office budget has more than doubled in the last four years from $4.2M in 2020 to $9.3M in 2024 , but his office is prosecuting fewer cases and winning a lower percentage of cases. Meanwhile County residents are facing a projected budget shortfall of greater than $300 million. If our Commonwealth's Attorney won't enforce the law and keep our community safe, his budget should be one of the first places we look to cut.

As I continue to seek the Republican nomination for Lieutenant Governor, I will continue to push for commonsense conservative solutions in Fairfax County as well as around the Commonwealth.

I appreciate your support both in Fairfax and across the Commonwealth and look forward to continuing to get to know you as the campaign moves forward.

Very truly yours,
---
| |
---
---
|
---
**Pat Herrity**
Springfield District Supervisor, Fairfax County
Candidate for Lieutenant Governor

---
| [Make a Contribution](https://patherrity.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=deffacbf7c4eb8be8b9889258&id=63cc81f5fe&e=a1ea0f9c99 "Make a Contribution")
---
|
---
| If you received this email as a forward, I'd love for you sign up for Herrity Headlines to receive these updates every week, directly from my campaign:
---
| [Sign Up for Herrity's Headline](https://patherrity.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=deffacbf7c4eb8be8b9889258&id=7fdde4940c&e=a1ea0f9c99 "Sign Up for Herrity's Headline")
---
|
---
| P.S. - Please save the date of March 16th for my annual Saint Patrick's Day Celebration in Fairfax County! We'll feature an exciting special guest, and the opportunity to learn more and get involved with my campaign for Lieutenant Governor this year!
---
| [RSVP for the St Patrick's Day Party!](https://patherrity.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=deffacbf7c4eb8be8b9889258&id=85af18597d&e=a1ea0f9c99 "RSVP for the St Patrick's Day Party!")
---

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://patherrity.us11.list-manage.com/profile?u=deffacbf7c4eb8be8b9889258&id=823194a417&e=a1ea0f9c99&c=516e83c329) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://patherrity.us11.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=deffacbf7c4eb8be8b9889258&id=823194a417&t=b&e=a1ea0f9c99&c=516e83c329)

name: Democrats.org
subject: Millions of people voted against Trump and his allies’ agenda:
body:
---
Derek, We are just a few weeks into Trump’s presidency, and we are already seeing the disastrous consequences. Trump and his billionaire allies are enacting plans that threaten our democracy and our freedoms. **But we are not powerless.** Millions of people voted against this agenda. There are millions of us — all across the country — who won’t stand idly by and let Trump and a Republican-controlled Congress do whatever they want. We can — and will — fight back. But to do that, we need your help. [**So we’re asking: Will you make your first contribution of 2025? We will put your contribution directly to work building the strong Democratic Party this moment demands.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-february-2025-member-card?amount=3&refcode=em_20250209_2730_fr&refcode2=2730_22930333_4w1daE&akid=2730%2E22930333%2E4w1daE) And when you contribute, you’ll receive your Official 2025 Democratic Member Card in the mail: [](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-february-2025-member-card?refcode=em_20250209_2730_fr&amount=3&refcode2=2730_22930333_4w1daE&akid=2730%2E22930333%2E4w1daE) We know the last few months have not been easy, and that there is a lot of work ahead of us — to not only mobilize and organize against Trump and his allies’ plans, but to build the long-term infrastructure we’ll need to elect Democrats up and down the ballot, in 2025, 2026, and beyond. **[CONTRIBUTE $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-february-2025-member-card?refcode=em_20250209_2730_fr&amount=3&refcode2=2730_22930333_4w1daE&akid=2730%2E22930333%2E4w1daE)** That’s why your help today is so important, and it’s why we’re so grateful to have you on this team. Thank you for everything, The Democrats

_If you gave recently via check or mail, our system may not have yet processed your gift. We are appreciative and thank you greatly for your support._
